etymology.description: 快(快) (kuài, "fast; quick; soon") + 點(點) (diǎn, "point; dot; a little") = speed up or do something quickly
etymology.interpretation: 快(快) (kuài, "fast; quick") refers to speed. 點(點) (diǎn, "point; dot; a little") originally refers to a small point or spot, but in this context, it is used to convey a small amount of something or an increment. Together as 快點(快點), it indicates adding a little speed, resulting in the meaning of "hurry up" or "do something quickly."

examples.chinese: 我要迟到了(我要遲到了)，快点(快點)！
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào chídào le, kuài diǎn!
examples.english: I'm going to be late, hurry up!

examples.chinese: 时间不多了(時間不多了)，我们得快点(我們得快點)。
examples.pinyin: Shíjiān bù duō le, wǒmen děi kuài diǎn.
examples.english: There's not much time, we need to hurry up.
